title: Cold Jellyfish Salad
description: This classic Chinese appetizer features tender, crunchy jellyfish strips tossed in a vibrant, savory-sour dressing. Its refreshing texture and balanced flavors make it a beloved starter, awakening the palate with every bite.

ingredients.notes: Optional, adjust to taste for heat

instructions.title: Prepare the Jellyfish (Soaking)
instructions.text: If using pre-salted jellyfish, rinse it thoroughly under cold running water for several minutes to remove surface salt. Then, place the jellyfish in a large bowl and cover with fresh cold water. Let it soak for at least 2 hours, changing the water every 30 minutes to further desalinate and rehydrate. This step is crucial for both flavor and texture.

instructions.title: Rinse, Drain, and Blanch Jellyfish
instructions.text: After soaking, drain the jellyfish completely. Rinse it under cold water one last time and squeeze out any excess water. Bring a pot of water to a rolling boil. Carefully add the jellyfish to the boiling water and blanch for no more than 15-20 seconds. Overcooking will make it rubbery. Immediately drain the jellyfish and plunge it into an ice bath to stop the cooking process and maintain its crisp texture. Once cooled, drain well and gently squeeze out excess water.

instructions.title: Prepare Vegetables
instructions.text: While the jellyfish is chilling, peel the cucumber and cut it into thin julienne strips. If desired, you can lightly salt the cucumber strips and let them sit for 5 minutes, then squeeze out excess water to ensure they remain crisp and don't water down the dressing.

instructions.title: Make the Dressing
instructions.text: In a small bowl, combine the minced garlic, light soy sauce, Chinkiang black vinegar, sesame oil, granulated sugar, and chili oil (if using). Whisk well until the sugar is fully dissolved and all ingredients are thoroughly combined. Taste and adjust seasonings if necessary – you might want a touch more vinegar for tang or sugar for balance.

instructions.title: Combine and Toss
instructions.text: In a large mixing bowl, combine the prepared jellyfish strips and the julienned cucumber. Pour the dressing over the jellyfish and cucumber. Gently toss everything together until the jellyfish and cucumber are evenly coated with the dressing.

instructions.title: Garnish and Serve
instructions.text: Transfer the Cold Jellyfish Salad to a serving dish. Garnish with toasted white sesame seeds and chopped fresh cilantro, if using. Serve immediately as a refreshing appetizer. The salad is best enjoyed fresh to preserve the jellyfish's unique texture.

tips: **Jellyfish Quality:** Always opt for good quality, well-prepared salted jellyfish. Proper soaking is paramount to remove excessive salt and rehydrate to the perfect crisp-tender texture.
**Blanching Time:** Be very precise with blanching time – 15-20 seconds is sufficient. Any longer will result in a rubbery texture, losing the signature crunch.
**Chilling is Key:** Ensure the blanched jellyfish is thoroughly chilled in an ice bath and well-drained before mixing to maintain its refreshing, crisp bite.
**Balance the Dressing:** Adjust the dressing to your preference. The balance of savory, sour, sweet, and a hint of spice is crucial for an authentic flavor profile.
article:
## History of Cold Jellyfish Salad

Cold Jellyfish Salad, known as Hǎi Zhé Pí (海蜇皮) in Mandarin, is a quintessential cold appetizer found across various regional Chinese cuisines, particularly popular in coastal areas where jellyfish is abundant. Its origins trace back centuries, with historical records indicating the consumption of jellyfish in China for its unique texture and purported health benefits. Traditionally prepared by fishermen and their families, the method of salting and drying jellyfish allowed for its preservation and widespread use. Over time, it evolved from a simple preserved ingredient to a sophisticated dish, celebrated for its refreshing qualities and distinctive chewiness, especially during warmer months or as a palate cleanser before richer courses.

## Why This Recipe Works

This recipe for Cold Jellyfish Salad excels due to its masterful interplay of textures and flavors. The jellyfish, when properly prepared, offers a delightful crisp-tender bite that is both unusual and highly addictive. This texture is perfectly complemented by the refreshing crunch of julienned cucumber. The dressing is a symphony of classic Chinese flavors: the umami depth of soy sauce, the complex tang of Chinkiang black vinegar, the aromatic richness of sesame oil, balanced by a touch of sweetness from sugar, and invigorated by pungent garlic and optional chili oil. Each ingredient plays a vital role, creating a harmonious balance that is savory, sour, slightly sweet, and aromatic, making it incredibly appetizing and memorable.

## Chef Tips for Perfection

To achieve the perfect Cold Jellyfish Salad, pay meticulous attention to the jellyfish preparation. Firstly, the soaking process is non-negotiable; insufficient soaking will leave the jellyfish overly salty and tough. Secondly, the blanching step is extremely brief – think of it as a quick dip rather than cooking. Over-blanching transforms the jellyfish from crisp to rubbery. Always have an ice bath ready to immediately chill the blanched jellyfish, locking in its desired texture. For the dressing, use fresh garlic and quality Chinkiang vinegar; these make a significant difference. Don't be afraid to taste and adjust the dressing to your personal preference for sweet, sour, or spicy notes. The goal is a vibrant, balanced flavor that enhances, rather than overwhelms, the subtle taste of the jellyfish.

## Serving Suggestions

Cold Jellyfish Salad is best served immediately after preparation to enjoy its optimal texture and freshness. It shines as a standalone appetizer, particularly well-suited to begin a multi-course Chinese meal. Its light and refreshing nature makes it an excellent contrast to richer, heavier main courses like braised meats or stir-fries. For an elegant presentation, serve it in individual small bowls or on a chilled platter. You can also pair it with other cold appetizers such as Smashed Cucumber Salad (Pai Huang Gua) or Marinated Tofu Skin (Liang Ban Dou Fu Pi) for a varied starter selection. A sprinkle of fresh cilantro not only adds color but also a burst of herbal freshness that complements the dressing beautifully.

recipeIngredient: 1 lb (about 450g) Prepared jellyfish (Pre-cut, salted jellyfish. Requires soaking.)
1 teaspoon Chili oil (Optional, adjust to taste for heat)
1 teaspoon Toasted white sesame seeds (For garnish)
